channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration flies 10-year-old back from Cuba amid custody fight
item.description: President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.

(Image credit: Hannah Schoenbaum)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
item.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.
item.content:encoded:
Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

(Image credit: Pavel Nemecek)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
item.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.
item.content:encoded: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
item.description: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.
item.content:encoded:
Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire
item.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.
item.content:encoded:
Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare/AFP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
item.description: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.
item.content:encoded:
The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
item.description: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.
item.content:encoded: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
item.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.
item.content:encoded: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
item.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.
item.content:encoded:
The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

(Image credit: Aamir Qureshi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
